params.name: Nipkow Disk
params.description:
On Aug 28, 1884 AD, the German inventor Nipkow invented the Nipkow disk.
It is a thick disc with small holes. It works by breaking an image into small dots and converting optical signals from these dots into electrical signals. These signals are then sent to a receiver to produce an image. This technology enabled simple image transmission, paving the way for the invention of TV.
params.skill1: [[File:Compass skill I.png|50px]]Place in Exploration Compass: Snail DEF +20
